title: Is the science of NERV the result of religion
display_title: Is the science of NERV the result of religion
wikitext:
[]: Is the "science" of NERV the result of religion?             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Thu Feb 21, 2013 3:06 pm This might sound like a crazy theory, and it only works within the universe of NGE itself. Okay, here it goes: what if the "religious symbolism" in the series is merely a reaction by the scientists (Ristsuko, Dr. Katsuragi, Gendo, Naoko, Fuyutsuki, and the three MAGI techs) as interpreting 2nd Impact as religious experience correlating to the Biblical interpretation of the apocalypse? Thus, everything that follows would be nominally tied to religion as result. Thus, instead of calling the Angels aliens,the Magi super computer, and Terminal dogma giant basement, they gave them these artificial names. Conversely, maybe giving their technologies these titles with such grandeur was indicative of their own (and humanity's) arrogant reliance on science to the point of being dogmatic and religious with it. Any thoughts? And yes, I know what Anno said about the symbolism, it looks "cool" and it's the occult in Japan... I'm talking about within the NGE universe itself...

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 21, 2013 4:03 pm Our science and religion are vastly inferior to that of the beings who created the seeds and left the scrolls, whose technology is not only so advanced it appears to be magic, it's so advanced it is magic. The ritual elements of the proceedings work without a real in depth understanding on the part of the characters for the same reason you don't really need to understand the principles behind a chemical reaction to make it work with sufficiently detailed instructions. The depth of occult symbolism in Eva goes way beyond the surface symbols of crossed shaped explosions and Western iconography, which distracts from it and is often used to dismiss it.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Feb 21, 2013 4:16 pm You make it sound as if the religious aspects are more important than they seem, when we are clearly told they are less so.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Feb 21, 2013 4:17 pm Well, things like the cross-shaped explosions, many of the angels, and even the Evas defy the laws of physics, at least as we currently understand them. So I guess you could fall back on the "technology sufficiently advanced as to seem indistinguishable from magic" explanation. What's interesting is that Nerv appears to be approaching Instrumentality, the angels, etc., from a purely scientific standpoint. Seele seems to take a much more religious bent, speaking in terms of "prophecies," scrolls, the divine, and so forth. It's left unclear whether Seele actually believes that God is involved in all of this, or whether they just speak in those terms out of an undiluted zeal for fulfilling what they believe to be the ineluctible course of human evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 21, 2013 5:05 pm pwhodges wrote: You make it sound as if the religious aspects are more important than they seem, when we are clearly told they are less so.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Thu Feb 21, 2013 5:16 pm Chuckman wrote: Our science and religion are vastly inferior to that of the beings who created the seeds and left the scrolls, whose technology is not only so advanced it appears to be magic, it's so advanced it is magic. The ritual elements of the proceedings work without a real in depth understanding on the part of the characters for the same reason you don't really need to understand the principles behind a chemical reaction to make it work with sufficiently detailed instructions. The depth of occult symbolism in Eva goes way beyond the surface symbols of crossed shaped explosions and Western iconography, which distracts from it and is often used to dismiss it.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 21, 2013 5:51 pm You've grabbed one of the essences of the show, yes. Nerv is a cult that sacrifices virgins to giant monsters to appease other giant monsters (after learning that they specifically needed virgin sacrifices- they tried it with mothers, who are by definition women who are not virgins, and it didn't work!) with the end goal of creating a messiah through an arcane ritual. Eva takes the metaphor of "playing god" from Frankenstein and makes it literal, subtext becomes text. Subtext becoming text is in itself a theme of Eva; it likes to set up little vignettes that take a commentary that's been offered and make it literal. The entire narrative is riddled with people trying to contextualize what is blatantly a religious/spiritual set of things and force them into a scientific, controllable framework. I don't have time to dig up a screen cap now but I'd refer you to the multiple shots of Shinji reading a book while the Evangelion looms in the background. The show is brimming with this theme.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Feb 21, 2013 6:02 pm Chuckman wrote: By who?

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Feb 21, 2013 6:30 pm I think it's always good to also point out that in-setting; religion, and all that other imagery/symbolism, would've just trickled down to the lilin subconsciously/subliminally from its FAR origins.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Feb 21, 2013 6:38 pm Well, I'm thinking of Kazuya Tsurumaki's remark that the Christian imagery is only there to look cool. But that seems to me to be true for the rest, in that the meanings of things like the Tree of Life, and so on in the series have no relationship to their supposed significance in historical human lore. The remark is well-known, and is quoted in the wiki : Kazuya Tsurumaki wrote: "There are a lot of giant robot shows in Japan, and we did want our story to have a religious theme to help distinguish us. Because Christianity is an uncommon religion in Japan we thought it would be mysterious. None of the staff who worked on Eva are Christians. There is no actual Christian meaning to the show, we just thought the visual symbols of Christianity look cool. If we had known the show would get distributed in the US and Europe we might have rethought that choice."

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Feb 21, 2013 6:55 pm Well, obviously. But that's why I was saying "in-setting" (i.e. in-story) as opposed to talking strictly aesthetics.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 21, 2013 7:14 pm I refuse to accept that Eva is so meticulously crafted and put together, except for this one part that's there for shits and giggles. The Western stuff means something in the context of the work; it's part of the work and shouldn't be written off or ignored. It's much the same as all the Freudian psychological terms; at first glance "Destrudo Release" looks like Engrish, but it means something. I've been working for a while on an occult and gnostic interpretation of Eva but it isn't finished yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Feb 21, 2013 8:04 pm Following in the footsteps of 2001: A Space Odyssey, Eva is all about the 'Nati's goal of evolving man to a higher form. These people use religious symbolism to denote philosophical "truths" for nearly everything. Youtube "William Cooper" and "Hour of the Time" or "Mystery School Babylon." You'd be blown away how quickly the things he's saying about the Illuminati relate to Seele, Nerv and Eva as a whole.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 21, 2013 8:15 pm Illuminati conspiranoia should resonate with Seele. Seele basically is a Western occult order of the "these guys run the world" type, right down to being behind the UN and screwing around with ancient aliens. They're clearly based on the Illuminati/Rosicrucians/Whatever. Again, it's more refined in Rebuild; the monoliths even have Pythagorean mystical mumbo jumbo on their backs.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Feb 21, 2013 8:18 pm ^ Exactly. What a lot of people don't know is that many of the great philosophers/mathematicians (like Pythagoras) of yesteryear belonged to these Mystery Schools, searching for the highest of understandings and god-like status... ... Just like Seele. Also, as you pointed out about Rebuild: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 21, 2013 8:46 pm A lot of it is religious terms taken and applied to FAR technology that fulfills a similar purpose. They aren't really the religious artifacts in question but their function is close enough that the name is apt. This was all clearly done intentionally.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Feb 22, 2013 9:08 am Merkaba wrote: ^ Exactly. What a lot of people don't know is that many of the great philosophers/mathematicians (like Pythagoras) of yesteryear belonged to these Mystery Schools, searching for the highest of understandings and god-like status... ... Just like Seele. Also, as you pointed out about Rebuild: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Feb 22, 2013 10:44 am Well, "cult" is just an exoteric term for "Mystery School." It's interesting that you bring up Satan, because the Mystery Schools (Illuminati) view Lucifer (interpreted as Satan and/or Jesus, depending on your interoperation of "Morning Star") as the rightful ruler of Earth, and that the monotheistic God was a cruel dictator who tried keeping Man as a slave in Eden. They looked to Lucifer/Satan because he bestowed upon Man the gift of knowledge, which beforehand was reserved for God and such.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Feb 22, 2013 4:12 pm While the religious imagery was adopted to look cool for the most part, they didn't sloppily incorporate it into the show; it's actually quite an essential part. The symbols and terms thrown about are accurate to their definition yet are taken out of the context of their origins in order to fit into NGE's own mythology, and it works. Yes, it's mostly for flash to make the show more exotic and there is no actual religious intent behind the usage, but the creators were aware of how to use them to best suit the spiritual journeys of the characters, whether they be in the form of the religious zealots, the power-thirsting scientists, or the lost children who are simply trying to find their find their reason to live. Merkaba wrote: Well, "cult" is just an exoteric term for "Mystery School." It's interesting that you bring up Satan, because the Mystery Schools (Illuminati) view Lucifer (interpreted as Satan and/or Jesus, depending on your interoperation of "Morning Star") as the rightful ruler of Earth, and that the monotheistic God was a cruel dictator who tried keeping Man as a slave in Eden. They looked to Lucifer/Satan because he bestowed upon Man the gift of knowledge, which beforehand was reserved for God and such.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Feb 22, 2013 6:29 pm To me, Sammael is Lilith in NGE's incarnation. She bestowed humanity with the Fruit of Knowledge, and since then, they're born into sin. Good call on that picture.
